Same outcome as question 1/2: refill valve activates, sprinklers don't run."],"confidence":0.97,"working":["Question 1: No rain detected, so timer activates pump at 6 AM. Pump draws from tank. Tank is at 15%, which is ≤20%, so pump activates refill valve instead of sending water to sprinklers. Even though it rained, pump still activates. Tank at 15% ≤ 20%, so pump activates refill valve, no water to sprinklers. Refill valve activates; sprinklers don't run."]}

final_answer: 1. No, the sprinklers do not run this cycle because the tank is at 15% which is below the 20% threshold.
2. Yes, the refill valve activates because the pump redirects to it when tank level is 20% or below.

3. Without the rain sensor, the timer activates the pump at 6 AM regardless of rain. With tank at 15% (below 20%), the pump activates the refill valve, and no water goes to the sprinklers. Same outcome as question 1/2: refill valve activates, sprinklers don't run.
working: Question 1: No rain detected, so timer activates pump at 6 AM. Pump draws from tank. Tank is at 15%, which is ≤20%, so pump activates refill valve instead of sending water to sprinklers. Sprinklers do NOT run.
Question 2: Since tank ≤20%, the refill valve IS activated to fill tank from mains.

Question 3: Without rain sensor, timer always triggers pump at 6 AM. Even though it rained, pump still activates. Tank at 15% ≤ 20%, so pump activates refill valve, no water to sprinklers. Refill valve activates; sprinklers don't run.
